elements.name: Title
texts: The monster guards a crock of gold

elements.name: Description
texts: "The same or a similar monster inhabits the lake yet (still) and was seen not many months ago. It guards a crock of gold which lies buried in the ancient island of the Purgatory…"

elements.name: Source
texts: Letter from John O'Donovan, Ballyshanny, 1st of November, 1835, p. 250, O’Donovan, John, Ordnance Survey Letters, Donegal: Letters Containing Information Relative to the Antiquities of the County of Donegal Collected during the Progress of the Ordnance Survey in 1835, p. 124

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Transcribed and edited by Michael Herity, MRIA

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Language
texts: English with Irish text in Celtic script

elements.name: Text
texts: "The same or a similar monster inhabits the lake yet (still) and was seen not many months ago. It guards a crock of gold which lies buried in the ancient island of the Purgatory. A Frenchman dreamed of this treasure some fifty years ago and came all the way from France to dig it out of the ground, but was of course prevented by the Ollphiast from carrying it out of so poor a country."

elements.name: Original Format
texts: Hand written letters held in Royal Irish Academy
